node_summary.purpose: Client-side JavaScript providing interactive behaviors: navbar scroll effect that adds visual feedback when user scrolls down, and smooth scrolling navigation for anchor links.
node_summary.behavior: 1. Script executes after DOM load (placed at end of body) 2. Scroll event listener attached to window object 3. On each scroll event, checks if scrollY > 50px threshold 4. If true, adds 'scrolled' class to nav element (triggers CSS styling) 5. If false, removes 'scrolled' class from nav element 6. querySelectorAll finds all anchor links with href starting with '#' 7. Click event listeners attached to each anchor link 8. On click, preventDefault() stops default jump behavior 9. getAttribute('href') extracts target section ID 10. querySelector finds target element in DOM 11. scrollIntoView() with smooth behavior animates scroll to target

key_data_flows.input: anchor href attribute provides target selector
key_data_flows.output: nav.classList toggled based on scroll position

side_effects.effect: nav element classList modified ('scrolled' class toggled)
Viewport scroll position changed on anchor click

important_patterns.pattern: Individual listeners on each anchor vs. single document listener Trade-off: More listeners but simpler code
if (target) { ... } prevents error when href points to non-existent ID
ES6 arrow syntax for concise event handlers
Modern classList.add/remove vs. className string manipulation

function.name: scroll-handler
function.description: Toggles 'scrolled' class on nav based on scroll position threshold
function.complexity: O(1) - single DOM query and comparison

function.name: smooth-scroll-handler
function.description: Prevents default anchor behavior and smoothly scrolls to target section
function.complexity: O(1) - single DOM query and scroll call

edge_cases.case: Non-existent anchor target: silently ignored via null check
Empty href="#": would return null from querySelector("#")
Rapid scrolling: scroll events may fire frequently (no throttling)
Reduced motion preference: not respected (always smooth scrolling)

performance.metric: Scroll handler fires on every scroll event (no throttle/debounce)
Single querySelector per scroll event (cached element would be better)
1 scroll + N anchor click listeners (N = number of anchor links)
performance.improvement: Cache nav element outside scroll handler to reduce DOM queries
Add throttle to scroll handler to reduce function calls
Consider IntersectionObserver as alternative to scroll listener

accessibility.concern: Smooth scroll may cause issues for users with vestibular disorders
